evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS fit + in-figure R²/slope/intercept annotation + OursModel highlight all present, directly matching brief requirements.
evidence.typography: Script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none'; pdffonts confirms Type 3 DejaVuSans-Bold/Oblique/Regular — default matplotlib font, not publication-grade.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4878CF) for models, red (#D65F5F) for OLS line, orange (#E8A838) for OursModel; blue/orange pair is colorblind-safe, but red on a fit line risks directional misreading.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,4.5) with tight_layout(); annotation box upper-right, legend upper-left — reasonable density, though both text elements compete in corners.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and provides numerical claims; however Type 3 fonts and DejaVu Sans default would draw journal/Reviewer 2 objections.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows all three fonts as Type 3 (not Type 42/TrueType); text is not selectable or editable in the PDF — critical vector fidelity failure.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27 KB), figure.png (73 KB), figure.svg (60 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numerical findings: slope=-0.0449, intercept=0.621, R²=0.877, and OursModel coordinates (log10N=9.449, loss=0.210) — fully informative.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; bonus SVG also generated.
Caption is quantitatively precise with slope, intercept, R², and OursModel coordinates explicitly stated.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 never set: PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts (DejaVu Sans), making text non-selectable and failing journal submission requirements.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout with no rcParams customisation — typography is not publication-ready and would be flagged by reviewers.
